basics.name: Kwang Hyun Kim
basics.summary: Ph.D. Candidate at UNIST specialized in atomic-level design and engineering of zeolite active sites (REACT). Expertise in heterogeneous catalysis for CO2 conversion and propylene epoxidation.

work.company: Korea Research Institute of Chemical Technology (KRICT)
work.summary: Participated in research on catalyst development for LOHC dehydrogenation.

education.institution: Ulsan National Institute of Science and Technology (UNIST)
education.summary: Advisor: Prof. Ja Hun Kwak. Passed dissertation proposal defense.

publications.name: Self-driven propylene epoxidation on modified titanium silicalite-1 by in situ generated hydrogen peroxide
publications.summary: Developed dinuclear Ti sites for stable epoxidation.

publications.name: Direct production of propylene glycol from propylene using bifunctional Al-TS-1
Selective Light Hydrocarbon Production from CO2 Hydrogenation over Na/ZnFe2O4 and CHA-Zeolite Hybrid Catalysts

presentations.name: Self-driven Propylene Epoxidation Based on In-situ Hydrogen Peroxide Generation
